extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a becomes true only via rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50): IF (elderly AND low_ejection_fraction AND qt_prolongation AND immunosuppressed AND pregnancy). rec_drug_b becomes true via rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50): IF elderly. The drug-a conjunction includes elderly as one of its conjuncts, so any input that fires the drug-a rule necessarily satisfies the drug-b trigger as well. The two rules target different outputs, so priority/conflict resolution within an output never suppresses one in favor of the other; both fire independently. With elderly=low_ejection_fraction=qt_prolongation=immunosuppressed=pregnancy=true: rec_a_from_conjunction sets rec_drug_a:=true (no competing rule for rec_drug_a, default not applied since it is set), and rec_b_from_trigger sets rec_drug_b:=true (no competing rule). Thus rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating mutual exclusion. The other inputs (hypertension, g6pd_deficiency, hepatic_impairment) only influence rec_drug_c and are set false for a minimal clean witness.
